items.title: Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute Overview
items.content: Institute Name: Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute. Institute Code: 59060. Type: Government. Established: 2008. Directorate: Directorate of Technical Education, Dhaka. Board: Bangladesh Technical Education Board (BTEB), Dhaka. Ministry: Ministry of Education (Technical and Madrasa Education Division). Program: Diploma in Engineering (4-year). Total students: 4200. Location coordinates: 24.450918 N, 90.859908 E, Kishoreganj, Bangladesh. Classrooms: 16. Workshops: 11. Labs: 17. Library: 1. Job Placement Cell: 1. Hostels: 2. Email: kishorepoly08@gmail.com. Website: https://kishoreganj.polytech.gov.bd/. Transport: 1 microbus.

items.title: Institute Location and Campus Description
items.content: The institute is located in Karimganj, Kishoreganj, on approximately 12 acres of land. It has classified labs including Basic Electronics Lab, Advanced Electronics Lab, Computer Lab, Chemistry Lab, and Physics Lab. It has practical workshops including Metal Shop and Basic Workshop. The institute has a Rover Scout team. In each semester, students receive CGPA on a 4.00 scale. The final semester is Industrial Training where students spend about six months in industry for practical learning. Around 65 percent of students receive government scholarship each semester.

items.title: Main Campus Infrastructure
items.content: The main campus includes a 4-storied building for academic and administrative activities. Facilities include offices, library, workshops, laboratories, conference room, and a 400-seat auditorium. There is a mosque on the north side of the main building and a Shaheed Minar in front. The campus includes a playground and flower garden with a natural and pleasant learning environment.

items.title: Technologies and Departments
items.content: Departments/Technologies: Electronics, Computer Science and Technology, RAC, Food, and Non-Tech Department.

items.title: Student Uniform
items.content: Uniform colors are sky blue and white.

items.title: Hostel Information
items.content: There is one hostel for male students and one female hostel.

items.title: Principal Information and Message (Updated 2025-03-17)
items.content: Principal (Additional Duty): Md. Mahbubur Rahman, Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute. Principal's message summary: Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute is a reputed technical education institute in Kishoreganj district. Students from Kishoreganj and distant areas study here. The institute has four technologies/departments: Computer Technology, Electronics Technology, Food Technology, and RAC Technology. Many graduates are working with reputation in government and private organizations, and many are also employed abroad. Academic quality and overall campus environment are described as very good. The campus includes a large playground, fruit garden, flower garden, and medicinal plants. The principal expresses continued wishes for the institute's success.

items.title: BTEB TVET Enrollment Analysis Report (Published August 2016)
items.content: Report title: A Report on Enrollment Analysis in TVET under Bangladesh Technical Education Board. Published: August 2016. Scope: enrollment analysis for TVET under BTEB compared with General Education Boards and Madrasah Education Board. Policy target referenced: TVET enrollment target 20% by 2020 (NSAPR II and NSDP policy context). 2014 enrollment findings: BTEB share in Secondary (Class IX-X) = 11.01%; Higher Secondary and Diploma = 16.18%; combined Secondary + Higher Secondary group = 13.11%. Projection: using incremental trends, projected BTEB enrollment reaches 20.82% by 2020 if other factors remain unchanged. Average incremental rates (2009-2015): BTEB 13.84%, General Boards 5.81%, Madrasah Board 4.24%. Other key facts in report: BTEB established by Parliament Act; located at Agargaon, Sher-e-Bangla Nagar, Dhaka-1207; affiliated institutes up to Dec 2015 = 7,773 with seat capacity 714,644. Source document: Enrollment_BTEB Report_Upload.pdf.

items.title: BTEB Enrollment Report Topics and Structure
items.content: Main topic blocks in the report include: government messages on skills and TVET, foreword/preface, BTEB objectives and structure, academic programs, TVET scenario in Bangladesh, enrollment methodology, 2014 enrollment tables, projection to 2020, findings and references, acronyms and definitions. The report discusses formal and non-formal training context, Gross Enrollment Ratio and Net Enrollment Rate definitions, and methodology using BANBEIS and BTEB Computer Cell data. It also includes curriculum-wise seat capacity tables and a National Technical and Vocational Qualification Framework (NTVQF) table.

items.title: Official Contact Page Details
items.content: Address: Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute, Karimganj Road, Karimganj, Kishoreganj (Bangla: Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute, Karimganj Road, Karimganj, Kishoreganj). Email: kishorepoly08@gmail.com. Phone: Registrar - 01992008084. Official Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/KishoreganjPolytechnicInstitute.

items.title: Institute Overview, Campus and Academic System (Expanded)
items.content: Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute is a government technical institute located in Karimganj, Kishoreganj, Bangladesh. Established in 2008, it runs a 4-year Diploma-in-Engineering program under Bangladesh Technical Education Board. Campus details: located beside highway area in Karimganj on about 12 acres, with 4-5 storied academic/admin building, library, workshops, 400-seat auditorium, mosque, Shaheed Minar, playground, and flower garden. EIIN number: 132756. Area noted as Jafrabadh, plain land zone. Major technologies: Computer, Refrigeration and Air Conditioning (RSC), Electronics, and Food Technology. Lab facilities include basic and advanced electronics labs, computer lab, chemistry lab, physics lab, and practical workshops. Admission: SSC/Dakhil/equivalent pass students can apply, selection by merit list. Program structure: 8 semesters, final semester is industrial training. Shift timings: 1st shift 08:00 AM to 01:15 PM, 2nd shift 01:15 PM to 06:15 PM. Student support: boys and girls hostels, rover scout, and government stipend (BDT 4000 per semester), with approximately 65-70 percent students receiving stipend. Official website: https://kishoreganj.polytech.gov.bd/. Mentioned recent notices include registration card distribution dated 04-01-2026 and examination notices.

items.title: Community Group Post Summary (Unverified Social Source)
items.content: This entry is from community Facebook group posts and should be treated as non-official unless verified from institute notice. Claimed points: established in 2008; location near Karimganj highway at Jangalbari, about 6 km south of district town; around 12 acres and green campus. Technologies: Computer, RAC, Electronics, Food under 4-year Diploma in Engineering (BTEB). Academic pattern: 8 semesters, 2 per year, final semester industrial training. Shift timings: 08:00 AM-01:15 PM and 01:15 PM-06:15 PM. Admission eligibility: SSC/Dakhil/equivalent by merit. Claimed student cost: about BDT 2000 per semester (session + form). Claimed stipend: BDT 4000 per semester with claim of 70% boys and 100% girls coverage. Claimed facilities: separate boys/girls hostel, library, mosque, Shaheed Minar, playground, pond, rover scout, labs (electronics/computer/chemistry/physics), auditorium around 400-500 seats. Group references mentioned: 'Tottho Batayon' (ID 600742636747364), 'All News' (ID 364311010926677), and 'Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute' (ID 537892633664811).

items.title: BTEB Role and Services Summary
items.content: Bangladesh Technical Education Board (BTEB) is described as the autonomous authority overseeing polytechnic institutes, vocational training centers, and diploma programs in Bangladesh under the Ministry of Education. It handles admission-related merit publication, examinations, results, and certification for government and private polytechnic institutions including Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute. Admission reference cited: btebadmission.gov.bd (example mention for 2026 admission cycle). Result references cited: eboardresults.com and bteb.portal.gov.bd. BTEB approves diploma-level technical courses such as Computer and Electronics under 4-year Diploma in Engineering structure. Kishoreganj Polytechnic is noted as operating under BTEB since 2008, with board-origin notices for admission and exams. General board update source mentioned: bteb.gov.bd.

items.title: BTEB Helpdesk Contacts (Official Site Snapshot)
items.content: From BTEB official site help-desk section: Admission helpdesk numbers listed as 01322-830308 and 01322-830309. E-service helpdesk listed as 01322-830279. Computer Cell contact listed as 01550620604. Users should verify latest hotline numbers directly on bteb.gov.bd before urgent use.

items.title: Bangladesh TVET Overview
items.content: Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) in Bangladesh focuses on practical, skill-based learning for industry and self-employment. Common pathways include SSC (Vocational), HSC (Vocational), Diploma in Engineering, and short course/certificate training. TVET aims to improve employability, support industrial growth, and reduce skill gaps in local and global labor markets.

items.title: Diploma in Engineering Structure (General Guide)
items.content: Diploma in Engineering at polytechnic institutes is generally organized in multiple semesters with theory and practical components in each term, followed by industrial attachment/training in the final stage. Students are evaluated through class tests, practical assessments, mid-term style internal assessments, and final board examinations.

items.title: TVET Career Readiness Guideline
items.content: For better outcomes after TVET studies, students should build both technical and soft skills: practical lab competence, project portfolio, clear CV, interview readiness, communication skills, and basic digital productivity tools. Participation in internship, job fair, and industry-linked training usually improves placement opportunities.

items.title: KPI Portal and Student Club Information (Official Site)
items.content: KPI official website navigation includes teacher portal and student portal links, plus admission/form-fill related sections. Club section on site includes Debating Club, Science Club, Computer Club, Blood Donation Club, and Cultural Club. This information is based on official website menu content and may be updated periodically by the institute.

items.title: Career Guidance Session Post by Sabiqunnahar Lija (Community Source)
items.content: Community post summary: Sabiqunnahar Lija shared a career guidance session announcement mainly for her students, especially female students aged 17-24. Focus areas mentioned: future education/training/work planning for female learners, effective CV writing, career path planning, self-development and workplace awareness. The session is described under UNICEF-funded GESP POWER project implemented by UCEP Bangladesh, Kishoreganj. Required participant information mentioned in post: Birth Registration or NID number, student/guardian mobile number, and date of birth. Post mentions light snacks for participants and requests sharing with female classmates. Contact number in post: 01760206532 (direct/WhatsApp). Treat as community-source information unless officially verified by institute notice.

items.title: Official Notice: 8th Semester Farewell and Industrial Attachment Payment
items.content: Office: Principal, Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute, Karimganj, Kishoreganj. Website mentioned: www.kishoreganj.polytech.gov.bd. Memo reference shown: 57.03.4842.301.201.02.0002.26-216. Date: 25 February 2026 (12 Falgun 1432). Notice summary: For all 8th semester students of 2021-22 session, farewell program scheduled on 05/03/2026 at 10:00 AM. Industrial attachment money to be distributed from respective departments. Students are instructed to submit ID card, 8th semester admit card, and receipt of BDT 600 paid to the attachment institution to the respective department head to receive payment. Signed by Md. Mahbubur Rahman, Principal (Additional Duty), Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute. Distribution list includes Vice Principal, Chief Instructors/Department Heads (Computer/Electronics/Food/RAC/Non-Tech), exam control room officer, section heads, notice board, and record file. Source provenance note: this information is primarily treated as an official office notice text; it was also circulated in a Facebook post attributed to Shakirah Khatun on 25 February 2026.

items.title: Consolidated Practical Routine Note (CST, Electronics, RAC, Food)
items.content: A consolidated schedule summary has been provided covering CST (2nd shift), Electronics (1st shift), RAC (1st shift), and Food Technology (1st shift) practical examinations for late February to early March 2026. Source context note: these routine details were shared in Facebook community posts, including a post attributed to Md Tafazzul in group/page context 'Tottho Batayon, Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute'. Treat as community-source unless matched with official institute notice. For precise exam preparation and attendance decisions, students should verify final timing, lab, and examiner details with the official departmental notice board and institute office.

items.title: Job Fair Update (ASSET Project Funding, Community Post)
items.content: Community post summary from 'Job Placement Cell KiPi': A job fair was organized at Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute with funding support from ASSET Project under the Directorate of Technical Education. The event was chaired by Principal Md. Mahbubur Rahman. The post states that many industrial organizations participated and that many students received job opportunities through the fair. This should be treated as community-source information unless cross-verified with an official institute notice or report.

items.title: KPI Job Fair Notice (31 January 2026)
items.content: Job Fair notice summary: Kishoreganj Polytechnic Institute announced a job fair under ASSET Project. Target participants: currently studying 8th semester students and passed alumni students. Date: 31 January 2026. Time: 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Instruction: interested participants should attend with their own CV/bio-data.

items.title: BTEB Rescheduled Diploma Exam Notice (February 2026)
items.content: Rescheduled exam summary for Diploma in Engineering: Theory exam originally suspended on 29 January to be held on 03 February 2026 (Tuesday) at 10:00 AM and 2:00 PM. Theory exam suspended on 30 January to be held on 05 February 2026 (Thursday) at 10:00 AM and 2:00 PM. Theory exams suspended on 31 January and 01 February to be held on 07 February 2026 (Saturday) at 10:00 AM and 2:00 PM. Practical exam revised period: 12 February to 18 February 2026 for all technologies, for 1st, 3rd, 5th, and 7th semester students. Second phase PF number online entry timeline: 24 February 2026 to 04 March 2026.

items.title: Diploma in Engineering Semester Plan 2026-2027 (Notice Summary)
items.content: Notice summary (Jan 2026 to Jan 2027): 2nd, 3rd, 5th, and 7th semester classes start from 08 March 2026. Mid-term exams for 2nd/3rd/5th/7th semester: 12-21 May 2026. Final theoretical exams for 2nd/3rd/5th/7th semester start: 22 July 2026. 3rd, 4th, and 6th semester classes and 8th semester industrial training start: 06 September 2026. Mid-term exams for 3rd/4th/6th semester: 10-21 November 2026. Final exams for 3rd/4th/6th and 8th semester start: 10 January 2027. 1st semester (session 2026-27) class start (probable): 15 September 2026. 1st semester mid-term: 10-21 November 2026. 1st semester final exam start: 10 January 2027.

items.title: Student Reapplication News for Stayed Exams (Community/News Source)
items.content: Community/news summary: Students from multiple polytechnic institutes reportedly submitted applications through their principals to BTEB, requesting re-exam scheduling for previously stayed exams held on 3, 5, and 7 February. The request mentions holding these exams from 22 February and completing practical exams quickly to reduce session delay. This should be treated as news/community-source unless confirmed by official board circular.

items.title: Education Minister Statement on Teacher Full-Time Duty (News Summary)
items.content: News-style statement summary: The Education Minister warned that action may be taken if teachers engage in other professions without prior permission from relevant authority, emphasizing teaching as a full-time profession and accountability in education quality improvement. Treat as news summary; verify exact policy implementation from official ministry circular.
